reset_note: Blind first-time run 2026-07-06 after operator wipe: 60 sourced -> four-trap verified -> 6 FUND.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.thesis: Trades 37% below hard net cash with a management that has a proven pattern of RETURNING capital (post-Janssen $2.47 special dividend plus repeated issuer tenders) — a rare micro-cap cash box that actually hands the cash back rather than burning it.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Uncovered micro-biotech priced below a countable net-cash floor; no analyst, no index, no forced buyer to close the gap.
dossiers.catalyst: Management's demonstrated pattern of special dividends + issuer tenders returns cash toward the net-cash floor.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: XBIT re-rates toward net cash (~$3.60/sh) within 12 months, or returns capital via tender/dividend.
dossiers.kill_condition: Net cash falls below market cap (floor gone) or a large dilutive raise.
dossiers.adversarial: Biotech cash boxes can burn the pile on a failed program; mitigant is the slow $9M/q burn (12.9Q runway) + the return history — but a pivot to an expensive trial would melt it.

verification.notes: Fresh blind run 2026-07-06; four-trap verified via primary filing by the fan-out.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Phunware — enterprise mobile software; now effectively a cash shell.
dossiers.thesis: Trades 54% below a $91.4M net-cash box with only $6.5M of total liabilities and no debt, barely burning (~35 quarters of runway) — the market has simply stopped watching a busted micro-cap that is now overwhelmingly cash on the balance sheet.
dossiers.why_mispriced: A busted micro-cap trading far below hard net cash the market has stopped watching.
dossiers.catalyst: Cash conversion / buyback / activist pressure to close a >50% net-cash discount.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: PHUN re-rates to at least 0.75x net cash within 12 months absent a large dilutive raise.
dossiers.kill_condition: A large dilutive raise or crypto/ATM pivot that spends the cash.
dossiers.adversarial: Capital-allocation history is the whole risk — a serial diluter can incinerate a cash box; this is why P(up) is only 0.42.

verification.notes: Fresh blind run 2026-07-06; four-trap verified via primary filing by the fan-out.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Identiv — post-divestiture RFID/IoT company, now a cash shell.
dossiers.thesis: Trades 45% below $114M of net cash following its divestiture; the flagged Series B preferred turns out to be pari-passu (participating, not senior) in liquidation, so the cash floor survives with roughly a 29% margin even fully diluted.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Post-sale cash shell the market hasn't re-rated; no coverage on the stub.
dossiers.catalyst: Redeployment of the divestiture cash / return of capital / small-cap re-rating.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: INVE re-rates toward 0.8x net cash within 12 months.
dossiers.kill_condition: Value-destructive acquisition or drawdown past the floor.
dossiers.adversarial: The cash could be spent on a bad deal (the classic post-divestiture risk); pari-passu preferred dilutes but doesn't strip the floor.

verification.notes: Fresh blind run 2026-07-06; four-trap verified via primary filing by the fan-out.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Imperial Petroleum — product/crude tanker owner (Greek FPI).
dossiers.thesis: Carries $530M of equity on just $16.6M of total liabilities (essentially debt-free tankers); current assets of ~$199M alone give a real net-net floor around $182M versus a $212M market cap, so you buy the fleet for almost nothing.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Obscure debt-free FPI tanker trading at a fraction of net-net book; no US coverage.
dossiers.catalyst: Fleet monetization / dividends / buybacks close a 60% discount to a near-cash balance sheet.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: IMPP re-rates toward net-net (~0.9x current assets) within 12-18 months.
dossiers.kill_condition: A large newbuild capex program that levers the balance sheet, or a tanker-rate collapse.
dossiers.adversarial: Shippers dilute and reverse-split; FPI governance is weak. But near-zero debt is the strongest defense in the batch.

verification.notes: Fresh blind run 2026-07-06; four-trap verified via primary filing by the fan-out.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.thesis: Trades at ~39 cents on the dollar of $420M GAAP equity backed by ceiling-tested PRODUCING oil & gas reserves, and — unusually for this bucket — the company is actually profitable (P/E ~17) with only modest leverage on the balance sheet.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Small profitable producer priced far below the book value of producing PDP reserves.
dossiers.catalyst: Reserve-value re-rating / buybacks / M&A in a consolidating small-cap E&P space.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: AMPY re-rates toward 0.7x book within 12-18 months absent an oil crash.
dossiers.kill_condition: A sustained oil-price collapse impairs the reserve floor, or a levering acquisition.
dossiers.adversarial: The floor is commodity-sensitive (medium, not hard) — an oil bear market marks down the PP&E; offset by current profitability.

verification.notes: Fresh blind run 2026-07-06; four-trap verified via primary filing by the fan-out.

dossiers.author: oracle
dossiers.business: Five Point Holdings — California master-planned community developer (Valencia, Great Park).
dossiers.thesis: Owns $2.48B of California master-planned land carried at HISTORICAL cost against only ~$111M net debt; Class-A tangible book is ~$1.22B versus a $577M cap, and the land is actively transacting ABOVE cost through ongoing homesite sales — a live catalyst that marks the hidden NAV.
dossiers.why_mispriced: Irreplaceable CA entitled land carried at decades-old cost, far below realizable value; the market prices the accounting book, not the land.
dossiers.catalyst: Ongoing Valencia/Great Park homesite land sales close above carrying cost, marking the hidden NAV.
dossiers.falsifiable_prediction: FPH re-rates toward 0.75x tangible book within 18 months as land sales validate the NAV.
dossiers.kill_condition: A California housing downturn stalls homesite sales, or a debt-funded land buy levers the balance sheet.
dossiers.adversarial: Land developers can sit at a discount for years without a hard catalyst; the mitigant here is that land IS transacting above cost now — but a housing recession would freeze it.

verification.notes: Fresh blind run 2026-07-06; four-trap verified via primary filing by the fan-out.
